refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these functions are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation aspect, which is a distinct functional requirement in the problem domain without implying any implementation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and specify performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context without reference to how these requirements are recorded or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository to document and maintain performance baselines and calibration updates
Incorporate safety and operational policy constraints into performance requirement derivation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical model that isolates the derivation of performance requirements from specific implementation details, supporting independence from other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk that safety and operational policies may require integration at this level, potentially coupling the DP with policy management functions
Maintaining traceability and history may require additional DPs or IMs not covered here
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for traceability and history management to fully meet acceptance criteria and reduce risk of coupling with policy management.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core logical processing and translation of worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters, serving as the central system intelligence.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data about the worksite and vehicle status to inform the logical model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context and task assignments from higher-level control or operator inputs.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if performance parameters indicate safety concerns.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with other modules and external systems for data exchange and coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the logical model is central to the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most data inputs and outputs are internal or through defined interfaces.
Vehicle module is more physical and operational; containing the logical model here would cause significant diffusion to other modules for mission context and performance parameters.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control aspects; logical model containment here would cause high diffusion and poor cohesion.
Safety Services could use performance parameters but is not the primary owner; containment here would cause moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the logical model translating mission context into performance parameters, minimizing diffusion and aligning with system architecture.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance parameters are tightly coupled.
Dependency on accurate and timely Telemetry and Tasking interfaces; interface failures could impact model accuracy.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by defining a model that translates the worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters, directly fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance parameters are tightly coupled.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating the logical model is well-contained within this IM boundary. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and maintaining documented history of baselines and calibration updates within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the logical model to the NGHE Solution module is clear and aligns with the system architecture and interfaces, supporting clear ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts and data exchange protocols with Safety Services to mitigate overlap and diffusion risks related to safety parameters.
Establish robust error handling and fallback mechanisms for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces to ensure model accuracy despite potential interface failures.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. However, given the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks with Safety Services, further decomposition into sub-FRs is warranted to isolate and manage safety-related performance parameters distinctly. This will help clarify interface contracts, reduce overlap, and improve error handling mechanisms, thereby mitigating risks and enhancing verification feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive sub-FRs to separate safety-related performance parameters from general performance parameters
Define sub-FRs for telemetry data handling and tasking interface robustness
Establish sub-FRs for error handling and fallback mechanisms in data exchange

revise.why: Clarify interface contracts and data exchange protocols with Safety Services to mitigate overlap and diffusion risks related to safety parameters, and establish robust error handling and fallback mechanisms for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces to ensure model accuracy despite potential interface failures.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module's high cohesion and low diffusion scores indicate a good architectural boundary for the logical model.
Safety Services module may have overlapping responsibilities with safety-related performance parameters.
Verification feasibility depends on traceability and documented history within the NGHE Solution module.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion risks with Safety Services regarding safety parameters.
Interface failures in Telemetry and Tasking could impact model accuracy.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts and data exchange protocols between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
Error handling and fallback mechanism specifications for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces.
